Package io.forja.builder

Class FxComponentBuilder<T extends javafx.scene.control.Control,B extends FxComponentBuilder<T,B>>

java.lang.Object
io.forja.builder.FxComponentBuilder<T,B>
Type Parameters:
T - the type of component this builder produces
B - the concrete builder subclass type
Direct Known Subclasses:
FxBadge.Builder, FxButton.Builder, FxCheckBox.Builder, FxChip.Builder, FxCode.Builder, FxColorPicker.Builder, FxComboBox.Builder, FxCopyButton.Builder, FxDropdownMenu.Builder, FxIconButton.Builder, FxKbd.Builder, FxLabel.Builder, FxLink.Builder, FxList.Builder, FxMenuBar.Builder, FxMenuButton.Builder, FxProgressBar.Builder, FxProgressCircle.Builder, FxScrollArea.Builder, FxSeparator.Builder, FxSplitButton.Builder, FxSplitView.Builder, FxTable.Builder, FxTabs.Builder, FxText.Builder, FxThemeToggle.Builder, FxToggleButton.Builder, FxTree.Builder, FxTreeTable.Builder, FxVerticalTabs.Builder
public abstract class FxComponentBuilder<T extends javafx.scene.control.Control,B extends FxComponentBuilder<T,B>> extends Object
Abstract base builder for all Forja component builders.

Provides the shared property surface common to every Forja component — id, disabled state, visibility, CSS style classes, inline style overrides, tooltip, and user data. Component builders extend this class and inherit all of these properties automatically.

The double generic pattern ensures fluent chaining works correctly across the inheritance hierarchy. T is the component type being built, B is the concrete builder subclass: 


 public static class Builder extends FxComponentBuilder<FxButton, Builder> {
     ...
 }

Every subclass must override build() and call applyBase(Control) on the constructed control before returning it: 


 public FxButton build() {
     FxButton button = new FxButton(text, variant);
     applyBase(button);
     return button;
 }
See Also:
  • io.forja.components.buttonsAndActions.FxButton.Builder

  • Constructor Details

    • FxComponentBuilder

      public FxComponentBuilder()

  • Method Details

    • id

      public B id(String id)
      Sets the CSS id of the component.

      The id is used to uniquely identify the component in CSS selectors. If an FxStyle is also set, it will scope its generated stylesheet to this id rather than generating a new one.

      Parameters:
      id - the CSS id, must not be null
      Returns:
      this builder

    • disabled

      public B disabled(boolean disabled)
      Sets the disabled state of the component.

      Defaults to false. When true the component is visually faded and non-interactive.

      Parameters:
      disabled - true to disable the component
      Returns:
      this builder

    • visible

      public B visible(boolean visible)
      Sets the visibility of the component.

      Defaults to true. When false the component is hidden but still occupies space in the layout.

      Parameters:
      visible - false to hide the component
      Returns:
      this builder

    • styleClass

      public B styleClass(String... classes)
      Adds one or more CSS style classes to the component.

      Classes are appended to the component's existing style classes — they never replace the component's own Forja style class. Classes are applied in the order they are declared, after the token layer and before any inline FxStyle override. 

      
 FxButton.builder()
     .text("Save")
     .styleClass("my-button", "compact")
     .build();
      Parameters:
      classes - one or more CSS class names to add
      Returns:
      this builder

    • style

      public B style(FxStyle style)
      Sets the inline style override for the component.

      The FxStyle is applied as a scoped stylesheet at the top of the Forja cascade hierarchy. Only properties explicitly set on the FxStyle are overridden — all other properties fall through to the token layer defaults. 

      
 FxButton.builder()
     .text("Save")
     .style(FxStyle.of()
         .background("#FF0000")
         .borderRadius(4)
         .build())
     .build();
      Parameters:
      style - the style override, or null to use defaults
      Returns:
      this builder

    • tooltip

      public B tooltip(String text)
      Sets the tooltip text shown when the user hovers over the component.
      Parameters:
      text - the tooltip text, or null for no tooltip
      Returns:
      this builder

    • userData

      public B userData(Object userData)
      Attaches arbitrary user data to the component.

      Accessible later via Node.getUserData().

      Parameters:
      userData - the object to attach, or null
      Returns:
      this builder

    • applyBase

      protected void applyBase(T control)
      Applies all shared base properties to the given control.

      Must be called by every subclass build() implementation before returning the constructed control. Properties are applied in the following order:

      1. CSS id
      2. Disabled state
      3. Visibility
      4. Style classes
      5. Tooltip
      6. User data
      7. FxStyle — applied last to respect cascade precedence
      Parameters:
      control - the control to configure, must not be null

    • build

      public abstract T build()
      Constructs and returns the configured component.

      Implementations must call applyBase(Control) on the constructed control before returning it.

      Returns:
      the built component
